Title: productConfiguration — Happy Path Two Required Product Sections
Preconditions: Runner-native. PUBLIC role (no auth required for productConfiguration). Uses CFG_LAPTOP (CFG-013) — two required Product sections (RAM, Storage).
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=get_config] errors[] empty
[DATA label=get_config] data.productConfiguration.configurationSections is non-null
[COUNT label=get_config] data.productConfiguration.configurationSections.length = 2
[DATA label=get_config] data.productConfiguration.configurationSections.0.type = Product
[DATA label=get_config] data.productConfiguration.configurationSections.0.isRequired = true
[DATA label=get_config] data.productConfiguration.configurationSections.1.type = Product
[DATA label=get_config] data.productConfiguration.configurationSections.1.isRequired = true
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] Both sections have non-empty options[] with product references and listPrice (runner-manual)
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty; configurationSections null/empty for known configurable product; options[] empty; section.type missing or wrong

Title: productConfiguration — Non-Configurable Product Returns Empty
Preconditions: Runner-native. CFG_HOODIE (CFG-003) is a plain Physical product (no configuration). Resolver should return empty configurationSections — NOT an error.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=get_plain] errors[] empty
[DATA label=get_plain] data.productConfiguration is non-null
[COUNT label=get_plain] data.productConfiguration.configurationSections.length = 0
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty for plain product; HTTP 500; configurationSections returns null instead of empty array

Title: productConfiguration — Invalid GUID — Graceful Empty
Preconditions: Runner-native. Zero-GUID configurableProductId. Resolver MUST NOT 500. Either errors[] non-empty with descriptive not-found OR empty configurationSections.
Assertions:
[DATA label=bogus_config] data is non-null OR errors[] is non-empty
[EVIDENCE] No HTTP 500 in network response
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] If errors[], message is descriptive (not stack trace); response time < 2s (runner-manual)
Failure_Signals: HTTP 500; raw stack trace in errors[]; resolver hangs

Title: productConfiguration — Text Section maxLength Returned
Preconditions: Runner-native. CFG_RING (CFG-017) has a single REQUIRED Text section with maxLength=30. Verify section.maxLength is returned to the storefront for client-side validation (PR #2235).
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=get_text] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=get_text] data.productConfiguration.configurationSections.length = 1
[DATA label=get_text] data.productConfiguration.configurationSections.0.type = Text
[DATA label=get_text] data.productConfiguration.configurationSections.0.isRequired = true
[DATA label=get_text] data.productConfiguration.configurationSections.0.maxLength = 30
[DATA label=get_text] data.productConfiguration.configurationSections.0.allowCustomText = true
Cross_Layer_Checks: [ROUNDTRIP label=get_text] storefront PDP enforces maxLength=30 in Text input (runner-manual via /products/configurable-hat path on CFG_RING)
Failure_Signals: maxLength returned null/0 for known Text section (would break client validation per VCST-4806); type wrong

Title: productConfiguration — Conditional Sections dependsOnSectionId
Preconditions: Runner-native. CFG_CONDITIONAL_BIKE (CFG-022) — chain A→B→C with sibling D (both depend on A). Verify dependsOnSectionId is populated for child sections so storefront can render conditional cascade.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=get_cascade] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=get_cascade] data.productConfiguration.configurationSections.length = 4
[EVIDENCE] At least one section has non-null dependsOnSectionId pointing at the root section's id (the root has dependsOnSectionId=null) — runner-manual
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] dependsOnSectionId values cross-reference sibling/parent ids in the response
Failure_Signals: All sections have null dependsOnSectionId (cascade lost); only 3 sections returned

Title: createConfiguredLineItem — Happy Path Preview With Required Selections
Preconditions: Runner-native. PUBLIC. Builds a preview ConfigurationLineItemType with both required Product sections selected for CFG_LAPTOP. Does NOT mutate cart. Captures section IDs from upstream productConfiguration query.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=create_preview] errors[] empty
[DATA label=create_preview] data.createConfiguredLineItem is non-null
[COUNT label=create_preview] data.createConfiguredLineItem.quantity = 1
[DATA label=create_preview] data.createConfiguredLineItem.extendedPrice.amount > 0
[DATA label=create_preview] data.createConfiguredLineItem.listPrice.currency.code = USD
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] Preview does not appear in /carts list — confirms it does NOT mutate cart (runner-manual)
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty for valid selections; extendedPrice 0 or null; price math diverges from base+option sum

Title: createConfiguredLineItem — Missing Required Section Returns Errors
Preconditions: Runner-native. CFG_LAPTOP has 2 REQUIRED Product sections. Pass empty configurationSections — expect validation errors[] non-empty. Mutation must NOT silently succeed (would break checkout invariant BL-CHK-001).
Assertions:
[EVIDENCE] errors[] non-empty OR data.createConfiguredLineItem is non-null with extendedPrice = base price only (no required sections counted) — accept either, but validation-first response is preferred per BL-CHK-001
[EVIDENCE] No HTTP 500
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] error message references missing required sections (runner-manual)
Failure_Signals: HTTP 500; resolver returns price WITHOUT noting required-section gap (silent BL-CHK-001 violation)

Title: addConfigurationItem + configurationItems — Roundtrip
Preconditions: Runner-native. ORG_USER auth. Adds CFG_LAPTOP to cart, attaches one Product configuration section (RAM base option), reads it back via configurationItems, asserts configurationItems[].sectionId matches the section just added. Cleans up with clearCart.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=add_item] errors[] empty
[ERRORS label=add_section] errors[] empty
[ERRORS label=read_back] errors[] empty
[DATA label=add_section] data.addConfigurationItem.items.0.configurationItems is non-null
[DATA label=read_back] data.configurationItems.configurationItems is non-null
[COUNT label=read_back] data.configurationItems.configurationItems.length >= 1
[DATA label=read_back] data.configurationItems.configurationItems.0.sectionId = {{SECTION_RAM_ID}}
[DATA label=read_back] data.configurationItems.configurationItems.0.type = Product
Cross_Layer_Checks: [ROUNDTRIP label=cart_query] re-query cart, verify items[0].configurationItems contains added section (runner-manual)
Failure_Signals: add_section errors[] non-empty; configurationItems read-back missing the section just added; sectionId mismatch

Title: addConfigurationItems Bulk + updateConfigurationItem Replace
Preconditions: Runner-native. ORG_USER. Add CFG_LAPTOP to cart, bulk-add both required Product sections (RAM + Storage) in one call, then updateConfigurationItem to swap RAM to a different option. Verify final state has new RAM option.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=bulk_add] errors[] empty
[ERRORS label=update_one] errors[] empty
[DATA label=bulk_add] data.addConfigurationItems.items.0.configurationItems is non-null
[EVIDENCE] After update_one, the RAM section's productId is OPT_RAM_UPGRADE_ID (not OPT_RAM_BASE_ID) — runner-manual
[COUNT label=bulk_add] data.addConfigurationItems.items.0.configurationItems.length >= 2
Cross_Layer_Checks: [ROUNDTRIP label=cart_query] cart query confirms RAM section has new productId post-update
Failure_Signals: bulk_add silently drops one section; update_one returns success but RAM section unchanged; configurationItems.length < 2 after bulk add

Title: updateConfigurationItems Bulk Update
Preconditions: Runner-native. ORG_USER. Build same starting state as CFG-GQL-009, then call updateConfigurationItems to swap BOTH sections in one round-trip. Verify both sections reflect the new productIds.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=bulk_update] errors[] empty
[DATA label=bulk_update] data.updateConfigurationItems.items.0.configurationItems is non-null
[EVIDENCE] data.updateConfigurationItems.items.0.configurationItems contains both SECTION_RAM_ID with productId=OPT_RAM_UPGRADE_ID AND SECTION_STORAGE_ID with productId=OPT_STORAGE_UPGRADE_ID (runner-manual)
Cross_Layer_Checks: [ROUNDTRIP] re-read configurationItems and confirm both updates persisted
Failure_Signals: bulk_update silently drops one of the two updates; sectionId mismatched in response; HTTP 500

Title: removeConfigurationItem + removeConfigurationItems
Preconditions: Runner-native. ORG_USER. Add CFG_LAPTOP, attach both sections, then removeConfigurationItem on RAM section, then removeConfigurationItems with both section ids (idempotent — second call must succeed even if RAM already gone).
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=remove_one] errors[] empty
[ERRORS label=remove_all] errors[] empty
[EVIDENCE] After remove_one, configurationItems no longer contains SECTION_RAM_ID; STORAGE remains (runner-manual)
[EVIDENCE] After remove_all, line item's configurationItems is empty or omits both sections (runner-manual)
Cross_Layer_Checks: [ROUNDTRIP] read configurationItems after remove_all — should be empty/null
Failure_Signals: remove_one returns success but section persists; remove_all errors when one section already removed (must be idempotent); HTTP 500

Title: changeCartConfiguredItem — Atomic Reconfigure With Quantity
Preconditions: Runner-native. ORG_USER. Add CFG_LAPTOP with quantity=1 + base RAM/Storage. Then changeCartConfiguredItem to set quantity=3 AND replace both sections with upgrade options in a single mutation. Verify final state matches expected.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=atomic_change] errors[] empty
[DATA label=atomic_change] data.changeCartConfiguredItem.items.0.quantity = 3
[EVIDENCE] data.changeCartConfiguredItem.items.0.configurationItems contains SECTION_RAM_ID with OPT_RAM_UPGRADE_ID AND SECTION_STORAGE_ID with OPT_STORAGE_UPGRADE_ID (runner-manual)
[DATA label=atomic_change] data.changeCartConfiguredItem.items.0.extendedPrice.amount > 0
Cross_Layer_Checks: [ROUNDTRIP] re-query cart and verify quantity=3 AND both sections show upgrade options
Failure_Signals: quantity not updated atomically with config change; config update silently dropped; HTTP 500; partial update (one of the two changes lost)
